attacking_style: Mountaintop finish specialist who reserves energy for final climb, responding controlled to attacks rather than initiating long-range moves. Relies on superior finishing power in final kilometers.
team_tactics: Insufficient data to establish clear team patterns across multiple races, though positioned well for stage finishes.
terrain_preference: High mountain finishes where sustained power and controlled accelerations matter. ITT strength provides safety net on GC battles.
vulnerability: Unknown across multiple race scenarios - single race data insufficient to identify repeated weaknesses.
key_patterns: Controlled race management: responds to attacks rather than initiating, saves energy for finish
Time trial confidence: willing to race defensively knowing ITT can decide GC
Bonus seconds awareness: tactical finish positioning to maximize 10-second opportunities
form_trajectory: positive: won multiple mountain stages and reached final ITT level on GC with race leader, showing consistent climbing form
